reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 21 cfs @ Fort Collins , 2 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir The warmer, spring-like weather this week has opened up some of the ice through the canyon. Below the Narrows, you’ll find some open and fishable water. Above the Narrows, focus on those south-facing runs they’re getting more sunlight and should be your best bet. Cold water temps are still keeping bug diversity and size down. Wherever you’re fishing around Fort Collins, expect small bugs. Think size 20–24 Midges and Baetis patterns. Try a Jujubee Midge or a Mayhem Midge. Keep an eye out for midge hatches and maybe even a few BWOs. A Charlie Craven’s Mole Fly or Shucking Midge can fool those trout that are sipping in the soft water. Spring is on the way whether we like it or not, so make sure your Baetis box is ready. Check out our BWO Tying Class to get stocked up. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment, a great Gateway selection for this time of year. If you need help finding fishable water or di
